title: Repro Launch Runbook
concept: A guided launch wizard for support engineers that treats every new Playground as a reproducible case: the first viewport is a step-by-step source, runtime, persistence, and handoff flow, while saved Playgrounds and Site Manager tools remain available as side tasks from the left navigation.
focus: left navigation with full-height task workspaces
blue green and amber operational states
side-task model for saved sites and Site Manager
flows: Create a Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, zip import, featured blueprints, or the full Blueprint gallery through the launch runbook.
Confirm runtime settings for WordPress version, older WordPress versions, PHP version, language, network access, multisite, and destructive reset or stored reload behavior.
Save temporary work to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress.
Browse saved and unsaved Playgrounds, resume them, rename saved entries, or delete saved entries.
Use Site Manager binder sections for settings, file browsing and editing, blueprint copy/download/run, database tools, logs, and exports.
Navigate the active WordPress path, refresh the frame, open Homepage or WP Admin, export to GitHub, download zip bundles, and import reproductions.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
